curses.textpad/halfdelay.name: halfdelay
curses.textpad/halfdelay.description: Used for half-delay mode, which is similar to cbreak mode in that characters typed by the user are immediately available to the program. However, after blocking for tenths tenths of seconds, raise an exception if nothing has been typed. The value of tenths must be a number between 1 and 255. Use nocbreak() to leave half-delay mode.

curses.textpad/has_colors.name: has_colors
curses.textpad/has_colors.description: Return True if the terminal can display colors; otherwise, return False.

info.excerpt: Return True if the terminal can display colors; otherwise, return False.

curses.textpad/has_extended_color_suppor.name: has_extended_color_support
curses.textpad/has_extended_color_suppor.description: Return True if the module supports extended colors; otherwise, return False. Extended color support allows more than 256 color pairs for terminals that support more than 16 colors (e.g. xterm-256color). Extended color support requires ncurses version 6.1 or later. Added in version 3.10.

curses.textpad/has_ic.name: has_ic
curses.textpad/has_ic.description: Return True if the terminal has insert- and delete-character capabilities. This function is included for historical reasons only, as all modern software terminal emulators have such capabilities.

info.excerpt: Return True if the terminal has insert- and delete-character capabilities. This function is included for historical reasons only, as all modern software terminal emulators have such capabilities.

curses.textpad/has_il.name: has_il
curses.textpad/has_il.description: Return True if the terminal has insert- and delete-line capabilities, or can simulate them using scrolling regions. This function is included for historical reasons only, as all modern software terminal emulators have such capabilities.

info.excerpt: Return True if the terminal has insert- and delete-line capabilities, or can simulate them using scrolling regions. This function is included for historical reasons only, as all modern software termin...

curses.textpad/has_key.name: has_key
curses.textpad/has_key.description: Take a key value ch, and return True if the current terminal type recognizes a key with that value.

info.excerpt: Take a key value ch, and return True if the current terminal type recognizes a key with that value.

curses.textpad/window/hline.name: hline
curses.textpad/window/hline.description: Display a horizontal line starting at (y, x) with length n consisting of the character ch.

info.excerpt: Display a horizontal line starting at (y, x) with length n consisting of the character ch.
